comprehensive_summary:
Alexander Pushkin (Alexander Sergeyevich Pushkin) is considered the founder of modern Russian literature and is its most canonical poet. He is most frequently clued through his verse novel Eugene Onegin, his narrative poem The Bronze Horseman, his short story 'The Queen of Spades,' and his Belkin Tales.

Eugene Onegin is by far Pushkin's most clued work, appearing in nearly every tossup. It uses a distinctive fourteen-line stanza of iambic tetrameter with alternating masculine and feminine rhymes (known as the 'Onegin stanza' or 'Pushkin sonnet'). The title character, the first literary 'superfluous man,' is a Byronic dandy who attends Tatyana's name-day celebration, where he flirts with Olga (Lensky's fiancee), provoking Lensky to challenge him to a duel. Onegin kills Lensky. Tatyana writes a love letter to Onegin, who gives her a condescending 'sermon' telling her marriage would bore him. Tatyana later realizes Onegin is 'a lexicon of words in vogue... mere parody and just a rogue' while reading his marginal notes. She reappears at a ball married to an old general named Gremin and rejects Onegin. The night before the duel, Lensky rehearses a poem 'like drunken Delvik at a feast' after reading Schiller. Tatyana reads her fortune in hot wax and dreams of dining with monsters after a bear chases her through snow. Vladimir Nabokov produced a controversial literal translation that led to a falling-out with Edmund Wilson. Charles Johnston's form-preserving translation inspired Vikram Seth's The Golden Gate. Dostoevsky's 'Pushkin Speech' praised Tatyana as the apotheosis of Russian womanhood and claimed she 'stood solidly on her own native soil.'

The Bronze Horseman is a narrative poem about the 1824 flood of the Neva in St. Petersburg. The protagonist Yevgeny (Evgeni) falls in love with Parasha, whose house is destroyed by the flood. Yevgeny survives by sitting atop a marble lion. He later goes mad and is chased through the streets by the title statue of Peter the Great. The poem contrasts Peter's 'eternal sleep' as nature-conqueror with the river's destructive power.

The Queen of Spades is Pushkin's best-known short story. The German engineer Hermann schemes to learn a secret gambling trick. The soldier Tomski tells of his grandmother, the 'Muscovy Venus,' who won a million francs on the advice of Count St. Germain. Hermann writes fake love letters to Lizaveta Ivanovna to gain access to the countess's house. He threatens the elderly countess with a pistol, causing her to die of fright. Her ghost forces Hermann to gamble his savings, and he goes insane repeating 'three, seven, ace' after losing to Chekalinsky when the title card appears.

The Belkin Tales (Tales of Ivan Belkin) are a collection framed as stories of the mysterious recently-dead landowner Ivan Belkin, inspired by Irving and Scott. 'The Shot' features the expert marksman Silvio, who rushes to finish a long-deferred duel with the Count but calls it off when the Count's wife stumbles in, shooting a Swiss landscape painting instead (the painting has two bullet holes). 'The Blizzard' features Burmin falling to his knees before Maria after realizing she is the woman he jokingly married during the title event. 'The Coffin-Maker' features a coffin-maker insulted at a shoemaker's dinner, haunted by skeletons. 'The Station Master' has a house decorated with the Parable of the Prodigal Son. 'The Squire's Daughter' ends with a student realizing his peasant love is Liza in disguise. Prosper Merimee translated and plagiarized 'The Shot.'

Pushkin's lyric poetry is also heavily clued. 'I Loved You' ('I loved you; and perhaps I love you still') is his most famous lyric. 'I Remember a Wonderful Moment' was written for Anna Kern. 'Ode to Liberty' led to his exile at age 18. 'The Prophet' describes a snake depositing a 'blazing shard of coal' in the speaker's chest before being told to 'burn Man's heart' with God's word. 'Winter Morning' ('Frost and sun - the day is wondrous!') is memorized by Russian schoolchildren. 'The Devils' features a coachman saying 'we've lost the way.' His 'little tragedies' include The Stone Guest (based on the Don Juan legend/Mozart opera), Mozart and Salieri (Salieri poisons Mozart's drink, confirming 'villainy and genius can never go together'), Feast in the Time of Plague, and Boris Godunov (a monk woos the Polish noble Maryna while leading a rebellion). Ruslan and Lyudmila features a 'learned cat' on a 'golden chain' to a 'green oak' and a hill that turns out to be the massive head of the wizard Chernomor's brother.

The Captain's Daughter is a novel about Peter Grinev stationed at Bailogorsk fortress, who quarrels with Shvabrin and pursues Maria Mironova. He is rescued from a blizzard by Pugachev, the historical rebel leader.

Biographically, Pushkin branded himself 'The African' due to his great-grandfather Abram Gannibal. He died in a duel with the French officer Georges-Charles d'Anthes, precipitated by anonymous letters naming Pushkin 'Deputy Grand Master of the Order of Cuckolds' after d'Anthes flirted with Pushkin's wife Natalia. Lermontov's 'Death of the Poet' commemorates his death, decrying 'haughty ones, descendants of forebears known for shallowness' and saying his 'blood of blackness will not obliterate true and righteous blood.' Pushkin started the journal The Contemporary with Gogol as editor.

works.name: Eugene Onegin
works.description: Eugene Onegin is Pushkin's verse novel and magnum opus. It uses the distinctive 'Onegin stanza' (14-line iambic tetrameter with alternating masculine/feminine rhymes). Onegin kills Lensky in a duel after flirting with Olga at Tatyana's name-day. Tatyana writes a love letter and is rejected, but later rejects Onegin at a ball.

clues.clue: Onegin kills Lensky in a duel after flirting with Olga at Tatyana's name-day celebration
quotes: the title dandy's duel
Lensky challenges the title dandy to a duel after Tatyana's name-day celebration

clues.clue: Tatyana writes a love letter; Onegin gives a condescending 'sermon' saying marriage would bore him; later Tatyana rejects Onegin at a ball, married to General Gremin
quotes: This character writes a love letter to a Byronic aristocrat who gives her a condescending 'sermon'
reappears at a ball married to an old general, where she rejects the man who killed Lensky

clues.clue: Uses 14-line stanzas of iambic tetrameter with alternating masculine and feminine rhymes ('Onegin stanza')

works.name: The Bronze Horseman
works.description: The Bronze Horseman is a narrative poem about the 1824 St. Petersburg flood. Yevgeny survives by sitting atop a marble lion. His beloved Parasha's house is destroyed. He goes mad and is chased by the title statue of Peter the Great.

clues.clue: Yevgeny survives a flood by sitting on a marble lion; Parasha's house destroyed; chased by the statue of Peter the Great
quotes: the protagonist sits on top of marble lions during a flood and stares at the title statue
Parasha dies in a flood... chased by the title statue

clues.clue: Contrasts Peter's 'eternal sleep' as nature-conqueror with the destructive river; set during 1824 Neva flood
quotes: contrasts the 'eternal sleep' of the nature-conquering Peter with a river that 'hurls itself' on the city

works.name: The Queen of Spades
works.description: 'The Queen of Spades' follows the German engineer Hermann, who schemes to learn a gambling secret. He threatens an elderly countess (the 'Muscovy Venus') with a pistol, causing her death. Her ghost forces him to gamble. He goes insane repeating 'three, seven, ace.'

clues.clue: Hermann goes insane repeating 'three, seven, ace' after the title card appears; German engineer loses fortune to Chekalinsky
quotes: going insane after losing a fortune on the title card
ends up in an asylum repeating 'three, seven, ace'

clues.clue: The soldier Tomski tells of grandmother, the 'Muscovy Venus,' who won a million francs on advice of Count St. Germain
quotes: Tomski tells a story about his grandmother, who sought out the Count of St. Germain
the 'Muscovy Venus,' who won a million francs

works.name: The Belkin Tales
works.description: The Belkin Tales are a collection framed as stories by the dead landowner Ivan Belkin. 'The Shot' features marksman Silvio finishing a deferred duel by shooting a painting. 'The Blizzard' features Burmin recognizing his jokingly-married wife. Other stories include 'The Coffin-Maker,' 'The Station Master,' and 'The Squire's Daughter.'

clues.clue: 'The Shot': Silvio finishes a deferred duel with the Count by shooting a Swiss landscape painting; two bullet holes
quotes: the expert marksman Silvio rushes off to finish a duel he started years ago
two old enemies take turns destroying a painting of a Swiss landscape

clues.clue: 'The Blizzard': Burmin recognizes Maria as the woman he jokingly married years prior during the title weather event
quotes: Burmin falls to his knees in front of Maria after realizing she is the woman he jokingly married four years prior

clues.clue: Collection framed as stories of dead landowner Ivan Belkin; inspired by Irving and Scott
quotes: a set of stories collected by the recently dead landowner Ivan Belkin
Inspired by Irving and Scott

clues.clue: 'The Coffin-Maker': insulted at shoemaker's dinner; haunted by skeletons of those he buried
quotes: several skeletons appear in a nightmare to an undertaker who had buried them

clues.clue: 'The Station Master': house decorated with Parable of the Prodigal Son; girl kidnapped by man pretending to be ill
quotes: a house decorated with an illustration of the Parable of the Prodigal Son falls into disrepair after a girl is kidnapped

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Pushkin's other major works include the 'little tragedies' (The Stone Guest, Mozart and Salieri, Feast in the Time of Plague), Boris Godunov, Ruslan and Lyudmila, The Captain's Daughter, The Transposed Heads, and various lyric poems including 'I Loved You,' the poem for Anna Kern, 'Ode to Liberty,' 'The Prophet,' and 'Winter Morning.'

clues.clue: 'I Loved You': 'I loved you; and perhaps I love you still' -- most famous lyric poem
quotes: His most famous lyric poem begins: 'I loved you; and perhaps I love you still'

clues.clue: 'I Remember a Wonderful Moment': love poem written for Anna Kern; 'like a spirit of the purest beauty'
quotes: the hard-to-translate line 'I remember that magic moment' begins a love poem that this author wrote to Anna Kern

clues.clue: 'Ode to Liberty': led to exile at age 18; tells monarchs 'neither flattery nor halters make sturdy barricades for you'
quotes: this poet's exile was blamed on his 'Ode to Liberty'
At age 18, this man wrote the poem 'Ode to Liberty,' which led to his banishment

clues.clue: 'The Prophet': a snake deposits 'a blazing shard of coal' in the speaker's chest; told to 'burn Man's heart' with God's word
quotes: the speaker's chest is ripped open by a snake, who deposits a 'blazing shard of coal'

clues.clue: Mozart and Salieri: Salieri pours poison, confirming 'villainy and genius can never go together'
quotes: pours poison in the rival's drink, confirming that 'villainy and genius are two things that can never go together'

clues.clue: Ruslan and Lyudmila: prologue describes a 'learned cat' on a 'golden chain' to a 'green oak'; a hill is the head of Chernomor's brother
quotes: a 'learned cat' bound by a 'golden chain' to a 'green oak'
a hill turns out to be the massive head of the brother of the wizard Chernomor

clues.clue: The Captain's Daughter: Peter Grinev at Bailogorsk fortress; quarrels with Shvabrin; pursues Maria Mironova; rescued from blizzard by Pugachev
quotes: a young officer quarrels with Shvabrin while stationed at Bailogorsk fortress
rescued from a blizzard by a mysterious man who turns out to be Pugachev

clues.clue: Boris Godunov: a monk (Grigory/False Dmitri) disregards Father Pimen's advice and woos the Polish noble Maryna while leading a rebellion
quotes: a monk disregards the advice of the former soldier Father Pimen and woos the Polish noble Maryna while leading a rebellion

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Pushkin is the founder of modern Russian literature. He branded himself 'The African' due to great-grandfather Abram Gannibal. He died in a duel with Georges-Charles d'Anthes after anonymous letters about his wife Natalia's flirtation. Lermontov's 'Death of the Poet' commemorates him. He started the journal The Contemporary.

clues.clue: Russian poet; founder of modern Russian literature; author of Eugene Onegin
quotes: name this Russian author of Eugene Onegin
this early 19th century founder of modern Russian literature

clues.clue: Died in a duel with Georges-Charles d'Anthes after anonymous letters naming him 'Deputy Grand Master of the Order of Cuckolds'; wife Natalia flirted with d'Anthes
quotes: precipitated by one man being satirically named Deputy Grand Master and Historiographer of the Order of Cuckolds
The victim's wife Natalia had flirted with this event's perpetrator, the French officer Georges-Charles D'Anthes

clues.clue: Lermontov's 'Death of the Poet' commemorates Pushkin; 'The Bard is dead!' and 'Fell, slandered by a gossip's dread'; 'black blood won't wash away the poet's sacred blood'
quotes: Lermontov's 'Death of the Poet'
'The Bard is killed!' references his death

clues.clue: Branded himself 'The African' due to great-grandfather Abram Gannibal; half-Brazilian parentage in fiction
quotes: branded himself as 'The African' due to his great-grandfather, the Black nobleman Abram Gannibal

clues.clue: Started the journal The Contemporary with Gogol as editor
quotes: started the journal The Contemporary as a financial venture that included Nikolai Gogol among its editors

cards.front: Poem: Tatyana writes a love letter to the title character, who gives her a condescending 'sermon' saying marriage would bore him; she later rejects him at a ball, married to General Gremin
Poem: uses a distinctive 14-line stanza of iambic tetrameter with alternating masculine and feminine rhymes ('Onegin stanza'); Charles Johnston's translation inspired Vikram Seth's The Golden Gate
Poem: Vladimir Nabokov produced a controversial literal translation leading to a falling-out with Edmund Wilson; included Notes on Prosody
Poem: Tatyana reads her fortune in hot wax and dreams of dining with monsters after a bear chases her through snow
Poem: the protagonist survives a flood by sitting atop a marble lion; his beloved Parasha's house is destroyed; the statue of Peter the Great chases him through St. Petersburg
Short Story: the German engineer Hermann goes insane repeating 'three, seven, ace' after losing his fortune when the title card appears
Short Story: the soldier Tomski tells of his grandmother, the 'Muscovy Venus,' who won a million francs on advice of Count St. Germain; Hermann writes fake love letters to Lizaveta Ivanovna
Short Story: the expert marksman Silvio finishes a years-long deferred duel by shooting a Swiss landscape painting instead of the Count; two bullet holes visible
Short Story: Burmin falls to his knees before Maria after realizing she is the woman he jokingly married years prior during the title weather event
Poem: the most famous Russian lyric opens 'I loved you; and perhaps I love you still'; the speaker hopes God helps the addressee find another 'who will love you as tenderly'
Poem: a love poem written for Anna Kern; the speaker says 'I remember a wonderful moment' and 'you appeared before me like a spirit of the purest beauty'
cards.back: I Remember a Wonderful Moment (Alexander Pushkin)

cards.front: Poem: prologue describes a 'learned cat' bound by a 'golden chain' to a 'green oak'; a hill turns out to be the massive head of the wizard Chernomor's brother
Author: died in a duel with Georges-Charles d'Anthes, precipitated by anonymous letters naming him 'Deputy Grand Master of the Order of Cuckolds' after d'Anthes flirted with his wife Natalia
Author: branded himself 'The African' due to his great-grandfather, the Black nobleman Abram Gannibal

summary: Alexander Pushkin (Alexander Sergeyevich Pushkin) is considered the founder of modern Russian literature and is its most canonical poet.
